title: Assesment of the visual stimuli properties in P300 paradigm
description: This dataset comprises 382 electroencephalographic recordings from 19 participants performing a P300 visual oddball paradigm with systematically varied stimulus properties. Participants completed three experimental sessions, each containing seven recordings with different numbers of visual stimuli (4-9) and flash types (standard flash or cartoon face overlay). EEG data were acquired from eight scalp electrodes at 256 Hz sampling rate, with detailed event markers encoding experimental phases and stimulus presentations.
methods_description: EEG recordings were collected from 8 channels (Fz, Cz, P3, Pz, P4, PO7, PO8, Oz) according to the 10-20 electrode placement standard, grounded to AFz and referenced to right mastoid (M2). Sampling frequency was 256 Hz. Participants performed 3 sessions with 7 recordings each, varying stimulus count (4-9) and flash type (standard flash or superimposed cartoon face). Event markers encoded experimental phases (Fixation, Target Presentation, Preparation, Stimulation, Rest) and stimulus identity.
